# EdgeBase Dart SDK (`edgebase`)

`edgebase` is the umbrella Dart package for EdgeBase.

It currently re-exports the Flutter client SDK surface, so client examples should follow `edgebase_flutter` semantics unless the user explicitly asks for a lower-level or server-side package.

## Package Boundaries

- `edgebase`
  Broad Dart entry point for app code
- `edgebase_flutter`
  Main client SDK for Flutter applications
- `edgebase_core`
  Low-level primitives such as HTTP, database refs, storage, field ops, and shared errors
- `edgebase_admin`
  Trusted server-side SDK that requires a Service Key

## Canonical Guidance

- Prefer `ClientEdgeBase(baseUrl)` for client-side code.
- Use `client.db(namespace, instanceId: ...)` for database access.
- Use `client.storage`, `client.functions`, `client.analytics`, and `client.push` from the client surface.
- Keep Service Keys out of Flutter and client runtimes. Switch to `edgebase_admin` for trusted code.
- When users ask for lower-level integration work, switch to `edgebase_core` types such as `HttpClient`, `DbRef`, and `StorageClient`.

## Realtime And Rooms

- Database live APIs use streams and are designed for Flutter-friendly reactive flows.
- Room APIs are client-side realtime features and belong on the Flutter/client path, not in admin examples.
- Preserve explicit `instanceId` handling for instance databases.

## Docs

- Flutter SDK: https://edgebase.fun/docs/sdks/flutter
- Database Client SDK: https://edgebase.fun/docs/database/client-sdk
- Room Client SDK: https://edgebase.fun/docs/room/client-sdk
- Functions Client SDK: https://edgebase.fun/docs/functions/client-sdk
- Analytics Client SDK: https://edgebase.fun/docs/analytics/client-sdk

## Install

- `dart pub add edgebase`
